mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing present valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in >65% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of instances where UNRATE spikes >0.3pp, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and accelerating consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term between GDPC1 growth and CPIAUCSL acceleration yields a negative coefficient in quarterly return regressions, or combined high-growth/high-inflation periods show positive 60-day returns >55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive equity returns over the next month when financial conditions are stable.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 during periods of stable credit spreads, or positive returns occur in <40% of rising INDPRO observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust consumer spending capacity and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in <45% of positive-payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation breaching the 3.5% annual threshold triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and declining housing starts signals a consumption-led economic shift away from interest-rate-sensitive sectors, reducing aggregate corporate profitability and generating negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS growth and HOUST decline yields a positive coefficient in quarterly return models, or periods of this divergence show positive 60-day returns >50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate occurring alongside a declining federal funds rate confirms a soft-landing disinflation trajectory, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling FEDFUNDS produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample regressions.
